title: LLM Observability — Drift Detection, Metrics & Observability at Scale
description: LLM observability is complex because outputs are unstructured and non-deterministic. Staff Engineers must build systems that monitor output quality, attribute costs, track model updates, and calculate statistical drift (PSI, KS test) in real-time.

sections.title: The Three Pillars of LLM Observability
sections.description: psychology Intuition: Observability requires mapping traditional telemetry (metrics, logs, traces) to LLM structures. Log input-output pairs; trace the RAG, tool, and safety checks; monitor tokens, latency (TTFT, TPOT), and cost.

sections.title: Statistical Drift Detection: PSI, KS Test & JSD
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Over time, model behavior changes due to provider updates or user base shifts. We detect this by measuring distribution changes using **Population Stability Index (PSI)**, **Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) test**, and **Jensen-Shannon Divergence (JSD)**.

bar_chart PSI threshold: PSI < 0.1 indicates no change, 0.1 to 0.25 indicates moderate shift, and > 0.25 indicates significant drift requiring retraining.
sections.codeBlock:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import ks_2samp

def calculate_psi(expected, actual, num_bins=10):
    # Helper to calculate Population Stability Index
    breakpoints = np.percentile(expected, np.arange(0, 100, 100 / num_bins))
    expected_cnt = np.histogram(expected, bins=breakpoints)[0] + 1e-5
    actual_cnt = np.histogram(actual, bins=breakpoints)[0] + 1e-5
    
    expected_pct = expected_cnt / sum(expected_cnt)
    actual_pct = actual_cnt / sum(actual_cnt)
    
    psi = np.sum((actual_pct - expected_pct) * np.log(actual_pct / expected_pct))
    return psi

def check_ks_drift(expected, actual):
    # Run KS-Test; small p-value (<0.05) indicates drift
    stat, p_val = ks_2samp(expected, actual)
    return {"drift": p_val < 0.05, "p_value": p_val}

sections.title: Feature Monitoring & Feedback Loop Detection
sections.description: psychology Intuition: In recommendation and chatbot platforms, models can create positive feedback loops (e.g. recommending items similar to what the user was shown, reinforcing bias). Monitoring feature distributions detects these spirals.

sections.title: LLM Service Level Objectives (SLOs) & Quality KPIs
sections.description: psychology Intuition: Traditional SLOs focus on server health (uptime, error rates). ML SLOs include **quality goals** (e.g. p95 Time to First Token < 300ms, refusal rate <= 4%, helpfulness score > 4/5 on hourly sampling).
sections.codeBlock:
def evaluate_hourly_quality_slo(scores, threshold=4.0):
    # Calculate percentage of samples meeting target score
    passing = sum(1 for s in scores if s >= threshold)
    return passing / len(scores) >= 0.95  # SLO target: 95% passing

details: Staff Engineer Summary: Observability requires statistical drift monitoring. Calculate PSI and KS test statistics on sliding inference windows, track feedback loop polarization via entropy checks, and establish model quality metrics as target SLOs.
